rulings.Case Title: JOWONNA BUTLER et al VS. EDGAR GARCIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Take Second Deposition Of Deft
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 09, 2013, line 1. PLAINTIFF BLYTH BUTLER, RICARDO GALVAN Notice Of Motion To Take Second Deposition Of Defendant. GRANTED. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH YODER et al VS. BRIAN VIGIL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs [Ccp 1008]
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 09, 2013, line 2. (part 1 of 2) DEFENDANT BRIAN VIGIL, KATHERINE SEARS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Motion To Expunge Notice Of Lis Pendens And For Fees And Costs [Ccp 1008]. DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden. The purported new facts do not directly address Court's finding that Plaintiffs have shown probable validity of their claim. Even if they did, Plaintiffs have submitted evidence to sustain their burden again. Plaintiffs shall recover attorney's fees in the amount of $2,337.50. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY A. NEEDELMAN VS. DEWOLF REALTY CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Application) To Reconsider And Modify Or Revoke Order Sustaining Defs' Demur To Plfs' 1st Amended Complaint W/O Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 09, 2013, line 3. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY NEEDELMAN, ONA NEEDLEMAN (Application) To Reconsider And Modify Or Revoke Order Sustaining Defendants' Demurrer To Plaintiffs' 1st Amended Complaint WithOut Leave To Amend. DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to sustain burden under CCP 1008. Plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 1005. Court declines Plaintiffs' suggestion to treat their motion as motion for relief under CCP 473, raised for the first time in reply. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN YU VS. CINDY YU et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, December 09, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT CINDY YU, TOSHIYUKI MUSHA DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege relationship that legally supports an unlawful detainer action. See Cal. Prac. Guide Landlord-Tenant Ch. 8-B, 8:40. = (501/REQ)
